title: 💻 Copilot Code Impact [Edu] [v2]
description: Measures the volume and quality of Copilot-assisted code contributions including lines suggested, accepted, acceptance rate, and PR attribution.

panels.description: Data mode banner — shows whether this dashboard displays live GitHub data (green), synthetic seed data (amber), or demo data (blue).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COALESCE(
  (SELECT
    CASE mode
      WHEN 'live' THEN '📡 Live Data' || COALESCE(' — ' || NULLIF(source_label, ''), '')
      WHEN 'seed' THEN '🌱 Synthetic Seed Data' || COALESCE(' — ' || NULLIF(source_label, ''), '')
      WHEN 'demo' THEN '🎮 Demo Environment' || COALESCE(' — ' || NULLIF(source_label, ''), '')
      ELSE '❓ Unknown Mode'
    END
  FROM data_mode ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 1),
  '⚙️ Awaiting first sync…'
) AS "Data Source"

options.content:
# 💻 Copilot Code Impact

**Supplementary Metrics — AI-Assisted Code Volume**

## What it Measures
This dashboard quantifies the volume and acceptance rate of code suggestions made by GitHub Copilot. It tracks lines suggested versus lines accepted across the 28-day window, and counts pull requests attributed to Copilot-active developers.

## Why it Matters
Acceptance rate and lines accepted are the primary signals of whether Copilot's suggestions are relevant and high-quality. Declining acceptance rates may indicate suggestion quality issues, mismatched context, or developers who have not yet learned to prompt effectively.

> ⚠️ **Attribution caveat**: A PR is counted as "Copilot-attributed" when the author has an active Copilot seat (activity within 28 days). This is a *proxy* — GitHub provides no per-PR Copilot telemetry. All Copilot attribution panels show this caveat.

## How to Interpret
| Metric | Healthy Signal | What it Means |
|---|---|---|
| **Acceptance Rate** | ≥30% | Suggestions are relevant and trusted by developers |
| **Lines Accepted** | Growing over time | Copilot is contributing meaningfully to code output |
| **Copilot-Attributed PRs** | High % of total PRs | Active users are shipping regularly |

panels.description: Copilot Code Impact — lines accepted, acceptance rate, and attributed PRs.

panels.title: Lines Accepted (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(loc_added_sum) AS "Lines Accepted (28d)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` → `loc_added_sum` | Tables: `copilot_org_metrics`

panels.title: Lines Accepted (28d) - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` → `loc_added_sum`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_org_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Sum of loc_added_sum across all daily rows in the selected date range from the org 28-day report.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **'Accepted' = kept at suggestion time** — a developer can accept a suggestion and immediately delete it. The metric counts the accept event, not code survival.
- **28-day rolling report** — the organization-28-day report always covers the last 28 days relative to today, not your Grafana time picker.
- **Org-level only** — no repo or team breakdown available.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- How much accepted code is still in the codebase today.
- Which repositories or teams generated the accepted lines.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(loc_added_sum) AS "Lines Accepted (28d)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Lines Accepted (28d).
Section divider.

panels.title: Lines Suggested (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum) AS "Lines Suggested (28d)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` → `loc_suggested_to_add_sum` | Tables: `copilot_org_metrics`

panels.title: Lines Suggested (28d) - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` → `loc_suggested_to_add_sum`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_org_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Total lines Copilot suggested (accepted or not) in the selected date range from the org 28-day report.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Same NDJSON pattern** — download_links → fetch S3 URL → parse NDJSON line-by-line (same as all Copilot Metrics Reports API endpoints).
- **Suggestion ≠ character saved** — a 1-line suggestion and a 20-line suggestion are both 'one suggestion'; loc_suggested counts the lines, not keystrokes.
- **Org-level only** — no per-repository or per-team breakdown from the organization-28-day endpoint.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Which languages or editors generated the most suggestions.
- Suggestions partially accepted (edited before committing).

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum) AS "Lines Suggested (28d)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Lines Suggested (28d).
Section divider.

panels.title: Acceptance Rate (%)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(
  SUM(loc_added_sum)::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0) * 100, 1
) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` (2026-03-10) → NDJSON → `loc_added_sum` / `loc_suggested_to_add_sum` | Tables: `copilot_org_metrics`

panels.title: Acceptance Rate (%) - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` (2026-03-10) → NDJSON → `loc_added_sum` / `loc_suggested_to_add_sum`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_org_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Sum of accepted lines divided by sum of suggested lines across all language breakdowns in the org 28-day report.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Multi-line suggestion bias** — accepting a 10-line suggestion counts 10 accepted lines even if the developer only wanted 2 and edited the rest.
- **NDJSON response pattern** — this endpoint returns a `download_links` envelope (not inline JSON). You must fetch the signed S3 URL and parse line-by-line.
- **Org-level aggregate only** — no repo-level or team-level breakdown from this endpoint.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Whether accepted code was later reverted or modified significantly.
- Acceptance rate by team, repository, or language.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT ROUND(
  SUM(loc_added_sum)::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0) * 100, 1
) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Acceptance Rate (%).
Section divider.

panels.title: Copilot-Attributed PRs

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT pr.id) AS "Copilot-Attributed PRs"
FROM pull_requests pr
JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

panels.description: Source: `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats` (proxy — no per-PR Copilot signal in GitHub API) | Tables: `pull_requests`, `copilot_seats`

panels.title: Copilot-Attributed PRs - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/pulls/pulls)

`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats` (proxy — no per-PR Copilot signal in GitHub API)

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`pull_requests`, `copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Count of merged PRs where the PR author has an active Copilot seat. ⚠️ Proxy metric — GitHub provides no per-PR Copilot usage signal.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **⚠️ Proxy metric** — GitHub does not provide a per-PR or per-commit signal indicating Copilot was used. Seat activity is the only available proxy.
- **Active seat ≠ used on this PR** — the author may have written this PR entirely without Copilot assistance despite having an active seat.
- **Seat revocation retroactivity** — if a seat is revoked after PR merge, the PR loses 'Copilot-attributed' status on the next sync.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- How much Copilot contributed to any specific PR.
- Code quality differences between attributed and non-attributed PRs.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT pr.id) AS "Copilot-Attributed PRs"
FROM pull_requests pr
JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Copilot-Attributed PRs.
Section divider.

panels.title: Lines Accepted vs Suggested over Time

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  day AS time,
  loc_suggested_to_add_sum AS "Lines Suggested",
  loc_added_sum AS "Lines Added"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` → daily rows | Tables: `copilot_org_metrics`

panels.title: Lines Accepted vs Suggested over Time - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` → daily rows

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_org_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Time series showing both loc_suggested_to_add_sum and loc_added_sum per day to visualize acceptance volume.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Rolling 28-day report boundary** — the organization-28-day report covers only the last 28 days. Extending Grafana's time range beyond 28 days shows no additional data.
- **No partial-day data** — each row is a complete calendar day. The current day's row may not appear until the next sync after midnight UTC.
- **Org-level daily totals** — spikes may be caused by one developer making many completions that day.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Day-of-week usage patterns.
- Per-user or per-language acceptance trends.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT
  day AS time,
  loc_suggested_to_add_sum AS "Lines Suggested",
  loc_added_sum AS "Lines Added"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Lines Accepted vs Suggested over Time.
Section divider.

panels.title: Acceptance Rate Trend

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  day AS time,
  ROUND(loc_added_sum::numeric / NULLIF(loc_suggested_to_add_sum, 0) * 100, 1) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API → `totals_by_language` JSONB array → `loc_added_sum` / `loc_suggested_to_add_sum` | Tables: `copilot_org_metrics`

panels.title: Acceptance Rate Trend - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API → `totals_by_language` JSONB array → `loc_added_sum` / `loc_suggested_to_add_sum`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_org_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Daily acceptance rate computed by unnesting the totals_by_language JSONB array using jsonb_array_elements() and dividing loc_added_sum by loc_suggested_to_add_sum.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **JSONB unnesting cost** — the query uses jsonb_array_elements(totals_by_language) to expand the per-language array before summing. This is more expensive than reading scalar columns.
- **totals_by_language vs totals_by_editor** — the report contains both arrays. They should sum to the same overall rate but may differ slightly due to rounding.
- **Day-to-day volatility on low-activity days** — if only a few dozen suggestions are generated on a quiet day, acceptance rate can swing from 0% to 100%.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Acceptance rate trend for a specific language or editor.
- Whether rate changes are driven by usage pattern shifts or suggestion quality changes.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT
  day AS time,
  ROUND(loc_added_sum::numeric / NULLIF(loc_suggested_to_add_sum, 0) * 100, 1) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Acceptance Rate Trend.
Section divider.

panels.title: Copilot PR Rate (%)

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT ROUND(
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN pr.id END)::numeric * 100.0
  / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT pr.id), 0), 1
) AS "Copilot PR Rate (%)"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

panels.description: Source: `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats` (proxy — no per-PR Copilot signal exists) | Tables: `pull_requests`, `copilot_seats`

panels.title: Copilot PR Rate (%) - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/pulls/pulls)

`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats` (proxy — no per-PR Copilot signal exists)

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`pull_requests`, `copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Percentage of merged PRs whose author has an active Copilot seat. ⚠️ This is a proxy — GitHub provides no per-PR Copilot usage signal.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Proxy, not direct measurement** — GitHub does not provide a signal on whether Copilot was used for any specific PR. Seat activity is the only available proxy.
- **Active seat ≠ used on this PR** — the author may have written this PR without opening Copilot at all.
- **Seat revocation retroactivity** — if a seat is revoked after a PR is merged, the PR loses its 'Copilot-attributed' status on the next sync.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- How much Copilot contributed to any specific PR.
- Code quality differences between attributed and non-attributed PRs.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT ROUND(
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN pr.id END)::numeric * 100.0
  / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT pr.id), 0), 1
) AS "Copilot PR Rate (%)"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Copilot PR Rate (%).
Section divider.

panels.title: Top 10 by Lines Added (Copilot Users)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  user_login AS "Developer",
  SUM(loc_added_sum) AS "Lines Added",
  ROUND(SUM(loc_added_sum)::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0) * 100, 1) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
GROUP BY user_login
ORDER BY "Lines Added" DESC
LIMIT 10

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` (2026-03-10) → per-user NDJSON rows | Tables: `copilot_user_metrics`

panels.title: Top 10 by Lines Added (Copilot Users) - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` (2026-03-10) → per-user NDJSON rows

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Top 10 Copilot users by total loc_added_sum from the per-user 28-day report — a separate endpoint from the org-level report.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Separate API endpoint** — this uses users-28-day/latest, not organization-28-day/latest. Same download_links → NDJSON pattern, but the per-user report has one row per user per day.
- **Privacy consideration** — per-user line counts are visible to org owners and billing managers. Individual developers may not be aware their per-user data is shown on this dashboard.
- **Lines added ≠ productivity** — a developer making many small completions (e.g., auto-completing import statements) ranks highly without writing more complex code.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Code complexity or quality of accepted lines.
- Whether high line counts correlate with faster delivery or fewer bugs.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT
  user_login AS "Developer",
  SUM(loc_added_sum) AS "Lines Added",
  ROUND(SUM(loc_added_sum)::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0) * 100, 1) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
GROUP BY user_login
ORDER BY "Lines Added" DESC
LIMIT 10
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Top 10 by Lines Added (Copilot Users).
Section divider.

panels.title: Copilot Seats Loaded

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Copilot Seats" FROM copilot_seats

panels.description: Source: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats` | Tables: `copilot_seats`

panels.title: Copilot Seats Loaded - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Row count of copilot_seats table. Used as a data health indicator alongside 'Last Successful Sync'.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Silent 403 failure** — if the sync server receives a 403 on the seats endpoint, it logs a warning and continues. The table stays at 0 rows. A 'successful' sync job ≠ seats data was loaded.
- **TRUNCATE + INSERT every sync** — unlike PRs and deployments (incremental), copilot_seats is fully replaced on every sync. A 403 leaves it empty.
- **Proxy for sync health** — use this alongside 'Last Successful Sync' to confirm data freshness; check server logs if this shows 0.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Whether seat data is accurate or stale.
- Breakdown by team or repository.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Copilot Seats" FROM copilot_seats
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Copilot Seats Loaded.
Section divider.

panels.title: Last Successful Sync

targets.rawSql: SELECT MAX(finished_at) AS "Last Successful Sync" FROM sync_jobs WHERE status = 'success'

panels.description: Source: Internal sync_jobs table (no GitHub API call) | Tables: `sync_jobs`

panels.title: Last Successful Sync - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source

Internal sync_jobs table (no GitHub API call)

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`sync_jobs`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

MAX(finished_at) from sync_jobs where status='success'. Shows when the last complete sync run finished.
### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **'Successful' sync ≠ all data loaded** — a sync job completes with status='success' while individual fetchers (e.g., copilot seats) fail silently. Always check records_synced per resource.
- **Timestamps in UTC** — finished_at is stored in UTC. Grafana displays in browser timezone; a sync at 00:00 UTC appears as yesterday in UTC-5 timezones.
- **sync_jobs is append-only** — each sync adds a row and the table grows indefinitely. No automatic pruning.
### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Whether all resources were successfully fetched (check records_synced column in the job record).
- Failure reason if the last job has status other than 'success'.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT MAX(finished_at) AS "Last Successful Sync" FROM sync_jobs WHERE status = 'success'
```

panels.description: Learning guide section for Last Successful Sync.
Section divider.
